$comment: Windy Chat ops surface — Class C, and the most instructive retrofit yet. Chat is a MULTI-SERVICE CONSTELLATION: ~13 Node services (directory, media, hub, backup, call-history, onboarding, push-gateway, social, translation) behind nginx path-routing at chat.windychat.ai, over a Synapse (Matrix) homeserver. FINDING: each service has its own /health + /version, but the nginx table routes them NOWHERE externally — only /_matrix/*, /_synapse/client/*, and /api/v1/{service}/* functional paths are exposed. So externally there is NO fleet-health aggregator and NO reachable per-service health. This is the biggest structural ops gap in the fleet. The HEADLINE punch-list item — a fleet-health aggregator endpoint that fans out across all services — is the new multi-service pattern (see docs/MULTI-SERVICE-OPS.md). Until it exists, this manifest honestly binds only what IS externally reachable: Synapse core liveness (Chat's heart).

get_health.note: Synapse homeserver liveness ONLY — the constellation's heart. A real Chat get_health needs the AGGREGATOR (see gap below).

get_status.note: per-service /version exist but are NOT nginx-routed externally; the aggregator should report every service's version.

get_capabilities.note: which services + bridges are up — this IS the aggregator's job.

get_logs.note: content-free only; NEVER expose message bodies (privacy hard line).

run_selftest.note: canary: send a message through Synapse + confirm a bridge delivers it.

reconnect.note: per-service; the aggregator could expose per-service reconnect.

restart_app.note: out-of-band (docker compose per service); ops plane outside the patient.

check_for_update.note: wire to the Steamroller fleet version manifest (L2).

reset_to_defaults.note: NEVER — Synapse holds real rooms + message history.

$headline_gap: THE fleet-health aggregator: one endpoint (e.g. GET /api/v1/ops/health) that fans out to every service's internal /health + Synapse, returns the constellation status {service: up|down|degraded, version}, and is nginx-routed externally + EPT-gated. This becomes get_health/get_status/get_capabilities at once. It is the FIRST thing to build for Chat and the template for every future multi-service Windy platform (see docs/MULTI-SERVICE-OPS.md).

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: Check whether Windy Chat's core is alive — the Synapse (Matrix) homeserver that carries every message and room. A 200 here means the heart is beating; if this fails, chat is fully down. NOTE: this checks only the homeserver core, not the ~13 surrounding services — a full constellation health view needs the ops aggregator (not yet built).
